notes: The Varo adaptor targets NO external HTTP API. Confirmed against ast.json, Adaptor.d.ts, README, and the compiled dist (no fetch/axios/http/request calls; only Map.get usage). It is a pure in-process data-transformation adaptor for vaccine cold-chain data, designed to run alongside the Gmail and Collections adaptors: it converts proprietary Varo/FridgeTag message contents (EMS-like text/JSON pulled from Gmail attachments) into EMS-compliant reports/records, and converts EMS reports back into Varo message components for emailing. EMS here refers to the WHO/PQS-style Electronic Monitoring/Management System temperature-monitoring data format. Because there is no upstream API, this OpenAPI document is a best-effort SYNTHESIS from the adaptor surface: all 5 public operations (convertToEms, convertItemsToReports, convertReportsToMessageContents, parseUtcForDataRange, isKeyInRange) are modeled as local POST transformation endpoints on a placeholder base URL (https://varo.local/transform) purely so the mocker can capture the input/output resource shapes (MessageContent, EmsRecord, EmsReport, ZReport, VaroMessageComponent, UtcRange). CAVEAT: there is no real server to mock; response schemas describe transformation outputs, not API resources.
